rag_rules: AGREE contracts for component ports must be placed in valid component type scopes, not implementation scopes
Implementation-level bindings and signal routing should be placed in implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections are defined
generic_rules: Use assign statements only for existing output features or declared auxiliary variables in implementation contracts
Guarantee statements should not be placed in implementation scope; they belong in component type contracts
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Component type contracts must relate visible outputs to visible inputs without referencing implementation-only subcomponent paths
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicit typed ports or constants are available
anti_patterns: Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scope; use component type scope for output dependency contracts
Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
irrelevant_topics: AADL property names as AGREE variables
AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
